draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer fluid, gesture-driven interactions over tap-heavy UI
facade.label: Smooth swipe
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors continuous motion, drag interactions, swipe navigation, momentum scrolling—minimize taps
facade.rejectImplies: Design favors discrete, snappy interactions with instant feedback; gestures are secondary to fast taps

evidence.hypothesis: Runners prefer fluid, gesture-driven interactions over tap-heavy UI
evidence.implication: Design favors continuous motion, drag interactions, swipe navigation, momentum scrolling—minimize taps

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to friendly, motivational language than clinical weather terminology
facade.label: Casual & encouraging
facade.acceptImplies: User values a conversational, supportive tone—app should feel like a running buddy, not a weather bureau
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers direct, factual information—app should strip away personality and stick to clear data delivery

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, forgiving visual surfaces for outdoor running—reducing harshness and eye strain in bright conditions
facade.label: Soft blur effect
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors organic, diffused aesthetics; gradual transitions; calming color palettes; matte finishes over glossy
facade.rejectImplies: Design should trend toward clarity, sharpness, and high contrast; crisp edges; digital precision; potentially cooler or more clinical visual language

facade.hypothesis: Runners need persistent access to key stats (pace, distance, weather alerts) while scrolling through hourly forecasts
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure: sidebar persistence vs. tabbed switching
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User wants always-visible stats panel + card-based content feed for weather details
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers full-width layout, possibly tabs or bottom nav, or dense list view over cards

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond to high-contrast, energetic colors that feel active and motivating
facade.label: Bright neon accents
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward bold, high-energy visual language—likely wants the app to feel fast-paced and stimulating
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers muted, natural, or subdued color palettes—likely values calm focus or minimalist aesthetics

facade.hypothesis: Runners need simultaneous route visibility AND real-time pace feedback; side-by-side layout beats stacked layers
facade.axisTargeted: Information hierarchy: parallel data streams vs sequential scanning
facade.label: Split screen: pace + map
facade.acceptImplies: User values spatial awareness equally with performance metrics during runs; app should allocate 50/50 screen real estate to navigation and stats
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes one stream over the other (either map-dominant or metrics-dominant); stacked cards or modal switching is acceptable; runners don't need simultaneous dual focus

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed weather views
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. straightforward, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, reassuring interface vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather data
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, immediate feedback when checking conditions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed weather views
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. straightforward, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, reassuring interface vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, immediate feedback when checking conditions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners appreciate tactile feedback through micro-animations that confirm interaction completion, especially after gesture input
facade.axisTargeted: Motion feedback specificity — abstract momentum vs. concrete impact confirmation
facade.label: Bounce on landing
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors perceptible micro-animations and impact feedback; subtle physics-based responses to gestures build confidence in gesture-driven interfaces for active users
facade.rejectImplies: Design favors invisible, instant state changes; motion should enhance flow without drawing attention to individual interactions; tactile skeuomorphism may feel childish or distracting during use

draft.title: RunWeather – Runner's Forecast Companion
draft.summary: A weather app designed specifically for runners, showing real-time conditions, air quality, and run-friendly forecasts with minimal distractions.
draft.html:
RunWeather

Conditions optimized for your run

72°F
72° Partly Cloudy
Wind
8 mph
Humidity
62%
Feels Like
70°
UV Index
6

Next 3 Hours

Now Partly Cloudy, 72° Good
2 PM Cloudy, 71° Good
3 PM Light Rain, 68° Fair

Air Quality

Moderate – Good for running
